title: Implemented scorer commands are omitted from the recognized-command error text
reasoning: The dispatcher has a working scorer adapter when commandTarget resolves to the scorer module, but the error message for other recognized-but-unimplemented commands lists supported commands without mentioning scorer. Users who hit an unimplemented recognized command get inaccurate CLI contract information and may assume scorer is not available.
recommendation: Include the scorer command names or a generic scorer reference in the supported-command message, ideally from the same command metadata used by commandTarget/cliHelpText so this cannot drift again.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for the CLI dispatcher or its user-facing error output.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CLI dispatcher test for a recognized unimplemented command that asserts the supported-command list includes every implemented adapter family, including scorer.
minimumFixScope: Update the recognized-but-unimplemented error message or derive it from centralized command metadata.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists, but the relevant lines moved. In current apps/cli/src/main/cli.ts, the scorer adapter is present at lines 74-83, and the recognized-command error text at lines 95-99 now lists the scorer lifecycle commands: init, score, accept, revert, status, cleanup. Current packages/orchestrator/src/main/orchestrator.ts lines 123-129 route exactly those scorer verbs to module "scorer", so the supported-command text no longer omits the implemented scorer command family. Existing test source in packages/orchestrator/src/test/orchestrator.test.ts lines 203-206 asserts those scorer verbs route to scorer, though I could not execute Vitest in this read-only sandbox because Vite/Vitest attempted to create temp files and failed with EPERM.
commands: rg -n "Supported commands:|recognized but its modernized runtime adapter|codenuke .* recognized" apps packages -S

node -e 'const {readFileSync}=require("node:fs"); const cli=readFileSync("apps/cli/src/main/cli.ts","utf8"); const errorLine=cli.match(/Supported commands: ([^`]+)\\n/)[1]; const supported=new Set(errorLine.replace(/\.$/,"").split(", ")); const scorer=["init","score","accept","revert","status","cleanup"]; console.log(JSON.stringify({errorLine,scorer,missing:scorer.filter(c=>!supported.has(c))}));'
